name: A curated list of tools implements the concept of Server Mesh
description: A curated list of tools implements the concept of Server Mesh

links.title: ambassador
links.description: Open source Kubernetes-native API gateway for microservices built on the Envoy Proxy

links.title: control-plane
links.description: Contains a Go-based implementation of an API server that implements the discovery service APIs defined in data-plane-api

links.title: dubbo
links.description: Apache Dubbo (incubating) is a high-performance, java based, open source RPC framework

links.title: envoy
links.description: An open source edge and service proxy, designed for cloud-native applications

links.title: istio
links.description: An open platform to connect, manage, and secure microservices

links.title: linkerd
links.description: A service sidecar for Kubernetes gives you instant Grafana dashboards and CLI debugging tools for any Kubernetes service — with no cluster-wide installation

links.title: mesher
links.description: A light weight service mesh implementation based on [go chassis](https://github.com/ServiceComb/go-chassis)

links.title: sofa-mesh
links.description: A solution for large-scale Service Mesh based on Istio

links.title: sofa-mosn
links.description: SOFAMosn is a modular observable smart network which can be used in service mesh deployed as a data plane sidecar

links.title: tars
links.description: Tars is a highly performance rpc framework based on naming service using tars protocol and provides a semi-automatic operation platform

links.title: Traefik
links.description: A reverse proxy / load balancer that's easy, dynamic, automatic, fast, full-featured, open source, production proven, provides metrics, and integrates with every major cluster technologies

links.title: What’s a service mesh? And why do I need one?
links.description: (2017-04-25) A service mesh is a dedicated infrastructure layer for making service-to-service communication safe, fast, and reliable. If you’re building a cloud native application, you need a service mesh

links.title: Service Mesh for Microservices
links.description: (2017-09-15) The most complex challenge in realizing microservice architecture is not building the services themselves, but the communication between services

links.title: Service mesh data plane vs. control plane
links.description: (2017-10-10) The situation summarized by the following series of tweets that Matt Klein wrote

links.title: nginMesh
links.description: (2017-12-07) NGINX Proxy in an Istio Service Mesh
